questions: Q1: Are there any remaining conflict markers in the resolved file or repo-wide?
Q2: Does the resolved tickets/index.ts correctly preserve both ticket and staging intents?
Q3: Will the TypeScript build pass with the resolved merge state?

answers.answer: No. Zero conflict markers found in src/tickets/index.ts or anywhere in the src/ directory. Direct grep verification confirmed 0 matches.

Grep <<<<<<< in src/ --include=*.ts: No matches found

answers.answer: Yes. The 150-line file has all 10 subcommand handler imports (lines 4-14), a switch statement dispatching to all 10 subcommands (lines 43-148), and proper ticket resolution using extractTicketRef and resolveTicket for ref-based commands.
evidence: Direct read of tickets/index.ts lines 1-14: 10 imports including list, latest, get, create, rerun, continue, artifacts, artifact, bundle, update-description
Direct read of tickets/index.ts lines 43-148: switch statement with 10 cases + default handler

statement: No code changes are needed for conflict resolution in helix-cli. The single conflicted file (src/tickets/index.ts) is pre-resolved with zero conflict markers.
